title: Deep lithospheric structures along the southern central Chile margin from wide-angle P-wave modelling
abstract: Crustal and upper-mantle structures of the subduction zone in south-central Chile between 42°S and 46°S are determined from seismic wide-angle reflection and refraction data using the Zelt & Smith ray-tracing method. Three TIPTEQ profiles (Corridors 2, 3, 4) were analysed along segments with oceanic plate ages of 14.5 Ma (Chiloé), 6.5 Ma, and 3 Ma (near Chile Triple Junction). The oceanic Nazca Plate is ~5 km thick with slightly increasing thickness northward. Three-quarters of trench sediments subduct into the subduction channel in south-central Chile. The updip limit of the seismogenic zone coincides with a strong lateral velocity gradient ~75-80 km from the deformation front interpreted as a palaeo-backstop.

location.description: South-central Chile margin between 42°S and 46°S (Chiloé to Chile Triple Junction), TIPTEQ SO181 cruise

seismic_lines.name: TIPTEQ Corridor 2 — ~42°S (14.5 Ma crust, Chiloé area), 350 km total, 75 km used here; TIPTEQ SO181
TIPTEQ Corridor 3 — ~44°S (6.5 Ma crust), 320 km; 45/50 OBS/H used; TIPTEQ SO181
TIPTEQ Corridor 4 — ~46°S (3 Ma crust, near Chile Triple Junction), 225 km; 34/37 OBS/H used; TIPTEQ SO181

data.name: SPOC Wide-angle OBS Seismic Data (three profiles, 36°–43°S)
data.description: Wide-angle OBS seismic refraction data from SPOC (SO161) and TIPTEQ (SO181) experiments. Three profiles across the south-central Chile margin used for P-wave forward modelling and 2D velocity structure.

data.name: Navigation:Primary
data.description: acquired during the Robert D. Conrad expedition RC2901 (1988)

data.name: Seismic Reflection/Refraction
data.description: acquired during the R/V Robert D. Conrad expedition RC2901 (1988)

processing.summary: Wide-angle seismic refraction data from three profiles along the south-central Chile margin (36°–43°S) processed through 2D P-wave forward modelling. Data from SPOC (R/V SONNE SO161, 2001) and TIPTEQ (SO181, 2004–2005) cruises. Velocity-depth models were derived by iterative forward modelling of travel times (Pg, PmP, Pn phases). Results constrain the depth of the Moho under the oceanic plate at varying ages (3–14.5 Ma at trench), and how thermal state influences the subduction geometry.
workflow: 1. OBS instrument deployment and recovery (SPOC SO161, TIPTEQ SO181)
3. Phase identification and travel time picking
5. Gravity forward modelling as additional constraint
6. Comparison of velocity models with thermal models
processing.notes: Primary data from TIPTEQ SO181 (Dec 2004–Feb 2005). Three corridors: Corridor 2 (~42°S), Corridor 3 (~44°S), Corridor 4 (~46°S). 64L (3900 cui) airgun array, shots every 100–150 m. OBS/H at 5.5 km spacing. Short 16-channel streamer also used for shallow structure. Legacy MCS from R/V Conrad RC 2901 (Lamont Doherty, 1988) used for decollement reflector (Bangs & Cande 1997). Open access PDF at Oxford Academic.

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. 8 authors confirmed (first names expanded from initials). DOI confirmed. CRITICAL location/profiles corrected: study area is 42°S–46°S TIPTEQ Corridors 2/3/4 (NOT SPOC 36°S/38.5°S as previously stated). vessel: 'R/V SONNE' → 'R/V SONNE (SO181, TIPTEQ, Dec 2004–Feb 2005)' + 'R/V Conrad (RC2901, 1988)'. year_acquired: 2001 → 2004. source_volume_cui: null → 3900 (64L × 61.02, paper states 3900 in³ directly). channel_count: null → 16 (short 16-channel streamer for shallow structure). Software: Zelt & Smith (1992) + Zelt & Forsyth (1994). Corridor lengths: 2=350km, 3=320km, 4=225km. OBS used: Corridor 3=45/50, Corridor 4=34/37.
